# create-ws-starter

> A CLI for scaffolding Next.js apps and Turborepo monorepos with batteries-included tooling: Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, Biome, and Vitest. The CLI is fully non-interactive when driven by an agent or CI — every prompt has a corresponding flag.

The CLI clones a starter template from GitHub, renames the package, installs dependencies, builds, and initializes git. When run without a TTY (e.g. by an AI agent or in CI) or with `--yes`, it never prompts — it uses the provided flags and defaults instead.

## Quick start

Scaffold a Next.js app (no prompts) — runs install, build, and git init by default:

```sh
npx create-ws-starter@latest my-app --yes
```

Scaffold a Turborepo monorepo (requires pnpm):

```sh
pnpm create ws-starter my-app --turborepo
```

## Options

- `[dir]` — positional argument: the app name and the directory to create. Must be lowercase alphanumeric with `-` or `_`. Use `.` to scaffold into the current directory. Supports scoped names like `@scope/app`.
- `--turborepo` — scaffold a Turborepo monorepo instead of a single Next.js app (requires pnpm). Default: Next.js single app.
- `--no-install` — skip installing dependencies.
- `--no-git` — skip initializing a git repository.
- `--no-build` — skip building the project.
- `--overwrite` — clear the target directory and continue if it already exists and is not empty. Required in non-interactive mode when the directory is not empty (otherwise the CLI aborts).
- `--pm <manager>` — package manager to use: `npm`, `pnpm`, `yarn`, or `bun`. Defaults to the package manager that invoked the CLI.
- `-y, --yes` — skip all prompts and use defaults / provided flags (non-interactive). Implied automatically when there is no interactive terminal.
- `-v, --version` — print the version.
- `-h, --help` — print help.

## Behavior notes

- Non-interactive mode is entered automatically when stdout is not a TTY (agents, CI) or when `--yes` is passed. No flag combination will block on a prompt.
- `--turborepo` requires pnpm; the CLI exits with an error if another package manager is selected.
- If the positional `[dir]` is omitted in non-interactive mode, the default name `my-ws-app` is used.

## Agent skill

This repo ships an agent skill that teaches coding agents how to drive the CLI
non-interactively. Install it into your agent with the `skills` CLI (https://github.com/vercel-labs/skills):

```sh
# add the skill from the repo (resolves the SKILL.md automatically)
npx skills add willsather/create-ws-starter

# or target the skill directory explicitly
npx skills add https://github.com/willsather/create-ws-starter/tree/main/.claude/skills/create-ws-starter
```

It installs to your agent's config directory (e.g. `.claude/skills/create-ws-starter/SKILL.md`).
List or search skills with `npx skills add willsather/create-ws-starter --list` and `npx skills find`.

## Templates

- Next.js app — https://github.com/willsather/nextjs-starter
- Turborepo monorepo — https://github.com/willsather/turborepo-starter

## Links

- Homepage — https://create.sather.ws
- Repository — https://github.com/willsather/create-ws-starter
- npm — https://www.npmjs.com/package/create-ws-starter
